LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/RBU * * * AFTER THE HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CLAIM FOR GENERAL DAMAGES ON THE MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE AND BATTERY CAUSES OF ACTION. THESE ARE SURVIVOR CLAIMS AND SUCH RECOVERY IS NOT ALLOWED. (SEE CCP 377.34.) THE REMAINDER OF THE MOTION IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. * * *

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S DEMURRERS TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/RBU * * * AFTER THE HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS:DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S DEMURRERS TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT (SAC) ARE OVERRULED.MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE CLAIM. THE SAC ALLEGES THAT DIGNITY FAILED TO OBTAIN HER CONSENT BEFORE PERFORMING THE MEDICAL PROCEDURE, CONTRARY TO ITS DUTIES UNDER 22 CRC 70707. (SEE SAC 14-15.) THIS SUFFICES. UNLIKE IN MOORE V. REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA (1990) 51 CAL.3D 120, 133, DIGNITY'S GATEKEEPING DUTY TO OBTAIN CONSENT REQUIRED NO MEDICAL TRAINING.BATTERY. THE SAC ADEQUATELY ALLEGES AN OFFENSIVE TOUCHING. (14-15, 29.) I CANNOT FIND WHETHER A REASONABLE PERSON WOULD HAVE APPROVED THE PROCEDURE AT THIS STAGE OF THE CASE. DISCOVERY MAY REVEAL WHICH DIGNITY STAFF MEMBERS DID THE ALLEGED TOUCHING. ELDER ABUSE. THE SAC ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT THE BATTERY WAS ELDER ABUSE. (SEE W&I CODE 15657, 15610.63.) WRONGFUL DEATH. PARAGRAPH 37 OF THE SAC ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT DIGNITY'S UNCONSENTED PROVISION OF ANESTHESIA CAUSED THE DEATH. ORDER SIGNED. * * *

Defendant Dignity Health'S Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. James Braden, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Reuben B. Jacobson of Lewis & Brisbois, Tel. No. 1-415-438-6612 appearing
for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendant Dignity Health'S Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint is taken under submission. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/rbu

* * * After the hearing, the court ruled as follows: The motion to strike is GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to the claim for general damages on the medical negligence and battery causes of action. These are survivor claims and such recovery is not allowed. (See CCP 377.34.) The remainder of the motion is DENIED. Order signed. * * *

Defendant Dignity Health'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S 2nd Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. James Braden, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Reuben B. Jacobson of Lewis & Brisbois, Tel. No. 1-415-438-6612 appearing
for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendant Dignity Health's demurrers to the second amended complaint
is taken under submission. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/rbu

* * * After the hearing the court ruled as follows:Defendant Dignity Health's demurrers to the second amended complaint (SAC) are OVERRULED.
Medical Negligence Claim. The SAC alleges that Dignity failed to obtain her consent before performing the medical procedure, contrary to its duties under 22 CRC 70707. (See SAC 14-15.) This suffices. Unlike in Moore v. Regents of University of California (1990) 51 Cal.3d 120, 133, Dignity's gatekeeping duty to obtain consent required no medical training.
Battery. The SAC adequately alleges an offensive touching. (14-15, 29.) I cannot find whether a reasonable person would have approved the procedure at this stage of the case. Discovery may reveal which Dignity staff members did the alleged touching.
Elder Abuse. The SAC adequately alleges that the battery was elder abuse. (See W&I Code 15657, 15610.63.)
Wrongful Death. Paragraph 37 of the SAC adequately alleges that Dignity's unconsented provision of anesthesia caused the death. Order signed. * * *
